title: Mars Mission Architecture Study
description: Design a complete human Mars mission architecture. Define the mission profile, vehicle requirements, crew systems, surface operations, and return plan. Compare your architecture with SpaceX's Starship approach and NASA's DRA 5.0.
whyItMatters: A human Mars mission is the defining engineering challenge of our generation. It integrates every discipline in rocket science — propulsion, structures, GNC, life support, mission design, and manufacturing — into a single programme. This is the capstone project that tests everything you have learnt.

steps.title: Define mission requirements
steps.description: Set crew size (4-6), surface stay duration (30 days vs 500 days), return requirements, and acceptable risk levels. These top-level choices drive everything.
steps.tips: Short-stay (opposition class) missions have higher delta-v but lower radiation exposure. Long-stay (conjunction class) missions are more efficient but require more surface infrastructure.

steps.title: Design the trajectory
steps.description: Select a launch window, compute the transfer orbit, and determine the total delta-v budget from Earth departure to Mars landing and back. Include all burns and aerobraking.
steps.tips: Use the 2035 or 2037 window — they have favourable geometry. Total delta-v with aerobraking: ~15-20 km/s depending on architecture.

steps.title: Size the vehicles
steps.description: Using your launch vehicle sizing tool, design the transit vehicle, landing vehicle, and ascent vehicle. Include crew quarters, life support, and ISRU plant.
steps.tips: SpaceX's approach: one vehicle does everything (Starship). NASA's approach: specialised vehicles for each phase. Compare both.

steps.title: Design surface operations
steps.description: Plan the ISRU propellant production (Sabatier process), surface habitat, EVA operations, science programme, and emergency abort options.
steps.tips: ISRU must produce ~100 tonnes of propellant over 26 months. This requires about 250 kW of power — a serious engineering challenge.

steps.title: Present the architecture
steps.description: Create a complete mission architecture document with timeline, vehicle specifications, mass budgets, cost estimates, and risk assessment. Present it as if briefing a programme review board.
steps.tips: Real architecture studies are 100+ pages. Aim for a thorough 20-30 page summary with supporting calculations.

deliverables: Risk assessment and comparison with SpaceX/NASA approaches

rubric.description: All mission phases covered from launch to Earth return
Calculations are correct and assumptions are justified
Multiple options evaluated with clear rationale for selections
Professional document suitable for an architecture review
